H-OS-L1.name: Pebble
H-OS-L1.sphere_plain: Highly effective for overstimulation; most notice improvement.

H-OS-L2.name: Rainfall
H-OS-L2.sphere_plain: Gentle overstimulation relief; listeners report noticeable change.

H-OS-L3.name: Glacier
H-OS-L3.sphere_plain: Provides calming effect; often used when trying to sleep.

H-AX-L1.name: Meadow
H-AX-L1.sphere: H‑AX‑L1 (Meadow) has n=67, mean=7.22, std=1.93, strong confidence. The distribution is right‑skewed with a secondary mode around 6‑7, indicating two responder sub‑clusters. Qualitative data (n=23) report 78% moderate‑to‑significant improvement, 10‑minute settle time, and balanced rhythm. AutoGluon predicts a higher anxiety score of 7.84, suggesting latent efficacy. YouTube reach is limited (7 views, no retention). The protocol appears robust but may benefit from targeting the lower‑scoring tail.
H-AX-L1.sphere_plain: Many feel calmer with anxiety after about ten minutes of listening.

H-AX-L2.name: Cottage
H-AX-L2.sphere: H‑AX‑L2 (Cottage) shows n=48, mean=6.94, std=1.62, meaningful confidence. The distribution is tighter, centering on scores 7‑8, though a modest bimodal settle‑time (5 min vs 20 min) appears in qualitative responses (n=20). Music opinions are split between liked and neutral, hinting at groove‑style effects. AutoGluon does not rank it as a top anxiety predictor. YouTube views total 6, indicating low discovery. Investigation of the longer settle‑time subgroup may refine the protocol.
H-AX-L2.sphere_plain: Provides steady anxiety relief, though some users prefer a quicker settle.

H-AX-L3.name: Valley
H-AX-L3.sphere: H‑AX‑L3 (Valley) records n=36, mean=7.08, std=1.89, meaningful confidence. Distribution exhibits a modest bimodal pattern (peaks at 6 and 8). Qualitative feedback (n=20) emphasizes very settling rhythm and a 10‑minute settle time. Cross‑run drift (sim=0.695) signals evolving interpretation. No AutoGluon prediction is supplied. YouTube views are 4, reinforcing its undiscovered status. The data suggest the deeper descent may engage a broader listener range.
H-AX-L3.sphere_plain: Effective for anxiety, especially after a ten‑minute settle.

H-CR-L1.name: Anchor
H-CR-L1.sphere: H‑CR‑L1 (Anchor) has n=43, mean=6.35, std=1.70, meaningful confidence. Scores cluster in the 5‑8 range, with 53% positive despite crisis entry expectations. Qualitative responses (n=24) include 33% reporting significant improvement and rapid 5‑minute settle times. AutoGluon predicts a higher crisis score of 7.96, indicating a discrepancy between observed and modeled efficacy. YouTube views are 6. Panel notes a possible need for a micro‑duration crisis variant.
H-CR-L1.sphere_plain: Helps acute distress for many, with quick settling for some listeners.

H-CR-L2.name: Harbor
H-CR-L2.sphere: H‑CR‑L2 (Harbor) presents n=33, mean=6.52, std=1.70, meaningful confidence. Distribution mirrors L1 but with a slight upward shift; 55% positive. Qualitative data (n=16) note strong rhythm settling and a split music opinion (neutral vs loved). AutoGluon predicts a top crisis score of 7.96, and cross‑bucket links to X‑ME‑L1/L2. YouTube views are 4. The protocol may benefit from groove‑specific tailoring for acute states.
H-CR-L2.sphere_plain: Supports crisis moments; listeners report strong rhythm settling.

H-CR-L3.name: Horizon
H-CR-L3.sphere: H‑CR‑L3 (Horizon) reports n=27, mean=6.52, std=1.91, meaningful confidence. The distribution is broader, with a notable tail of immediate settle‑time responses (37% within 2 min). Qualitative feedback (n=19) includes mixed music opinions and a few low scores, reflecting heterogeneity. No AutoGluon prediction is provided. YouTube views are 5. The rapid entrainment suggests a design strength worth probing further.
H-CR-L3.sphere_plain: Rapidly stabilizes some users, but music fit varies.

H-SL-L1.name: Lantern
H-SL-L1.sphere_plain: Helps many fall asleep quickly; rhythm is mostly comfortable.

H-SL-L2.name: Hammock
H-SL-L2.sphere_plain: Consistently supports sleep; a few find rhythm a bit busy.

H-SL-L3.name: Midnight
H-SL-L3.sphere_plain: Works well for rest; some notice slight busyness.

T-FC-L1.name: Compass
T-FC-L1.sphere_plain: Helps focus for some, but a few feel the rhythm is too busy.

T-FC-L2.name: Waypoint
T-FC-L2.sphere_plain: Improves focus for most, with a comfortable rhythm.

T-FC-L3.name: Summit
T-FC-L3.sphere_plain: Strong focus aid for many, though experiences vary widely.

T-CF-L1.name: Ember
T-CF-L1.sphere_plain: Offers a modest creative boost; listeners settle in quickly.

T-CF-L2.name: Current
T-CF-L2.sphere_plain: Provides a steady creative flow with liked music.

T-CF-L3.name: Aurora
T-CF-L3.sphere_plain: Delivers a solid creative lift; many report noticeable benefit.

T-MB-L1.name: Stone
T-MB-L1.sphere_plain: Supports balance and maintenance well, especially in longer sessions.

T-MB-L2.name: Axis
T-MB-L2.sphere_plain: Works for balance but some listeners struggle early on.

T-MB-L3.name: Orbit
T-MB-L3.sphere_plain: Helps maintain balance, but a few don’t feel settled.

X-ME-L1.name: Breeze
X-ME-L1.sphere: X‑ME‑L1 (Breeze) shows n=39, mean=6.74, std=1.94, meaningful confidence. Distribution spans the mid‑range; qualitative (n=22) notes strong music love and balanced rhythm. AutoGluon predicts the highest zone score (8.23), indicating latent efficacy across multiple zones. YouTube views are 8. The protocol is a strong candidate for universal entry testing.
X-ME-L1.sphere_plain: Boosts mood and energy for many, and listeners often love the music.

X-ME-L2.name: Sunrise
X-ME-L2.sphere: X‑ME‑L2 (Sunrise) records n=25, mean=6.76, std=1.71, meaningful confidence. Distribution similar to L1; qualitative (n=15) reports significant improvement and high music likability. AutoGluon predicts 8.23 as well. YouTube views are 8. Consider promoting as a complement to L1 for varied listener preferences.
X-ME-L2.sphere_plain: Consistently lifts mood; listeners rate it highly.

X-ME-L3.name: Updraft
X-ME-L3.sphere: X‑ME‑L3 (Updraft) has n=18, mean=6.28, std=1.74, developing confidence. Distribution is slightly lower with some neutral responses; qualitative (n=25) shows mixed improvement and balanced rhythm. AutoGluon predicts 8.23, a notable upward delta, suggesting potential under‑scoring. YouTube views are 6. A longer hold or deeper ascent may unlock predicted benefits.
X-ME-L3.sphere_plain: Mixed mood impact; some enjoy it, others feel neutral.

X-MD-L1.name: Footpath
X-MD-L1.sphere: X‑MD‑L1 (Footpath) presents n=22, mean=6.36, std=2.01, meaningful confidence. Distribution is broad, with a low‑score tail; qualitative (n=17) shows improvement but modest rhythm satisfaction. AutoGluon predicts 7.88, higher than observed, suggesting under‑performance. YouTube views are 5. Extending the hold phase could capture the higher‑predicted potential.
X-MD-L1.sphere_plain: Motivation boost for some, but many see modest gains.

X-MD-L2.name: Trail
X-MD-L2.sphere: X‑MD‑L2 (Trail) has n=14, mean=6.21, std=1.63, developing confidence. Distribution leans low; qualitative (n=21) indicates modest improvement and mixed rhythm feedback. No AutoGluon prediction. YouTube views are 7. Enhancing early rhythmic engagement may raise outcomes.
X-MD-L2.sphere_plain: Provides a slight motivation lift; reactions vary.

X-MD-L3.name: Switchback
X-MD-L3.sphere: X‑MD‑L3 (Switchback) records n=13, mean=7.15, std=2.38, developing confidence. Distribution is bimodal with 15% scoring 10 and 15% ≤3. Qualitative (n=17) reports moderate improvement and balanced rhythm. AutoGluon predicts 7.88, close to observed high end. YouTube views are 7. Splitting into gentler and steeper ascent variants could resolve the bimodality.
X-MD-L3.sphere_plain: Can spark strong motivation for a subset, while others see little change.

X-MC-L1.name: Cavern
X-MC-L1.sphere: X‑MC‑L1 (Cavern) has n=24, mean=6.75, std=1.62, meaningful confidence. Distribution centers on 6‑8 with a few low scores; qualitative (n=25) includes two reports of worsening clarity. Rhythm is balanced, music loved. AutoGluon predicts 7.54, indicating room for improvement. YouTube views are 8. Consider adding a pre‑hold phase to mitigate disorientation.
X-MC-L1.sphere_plain: Helps clarity for many, though a couple felt worse.

X-MC-L2.name: Starlight
X-MC-L2.sphere: X‑MC‑L2 (Starlight) shows n=16, mean=6.81, std=1.64, developing confidence. Distribution is modestly right‑skewed; qualitative (n=20) records significant improvement for many and strong rhythm settling. AutoGluon predicts 7.54, above observed. YouTube views are 12. The protocol appears promising; longer exposure may raise scores.
X-MC-L2.sphere_plain: Generally improves mental clarity with liked music.

X-MC-L3.name: Cosmos
X-MC-L3.sphere: X‑MC‑L3 (Cosmos) records n=10, mean=5.70, std=2.45, developing confidence. Distribution is bimodal with 30% ≤3 and 50% positive, reflecting a strong population split. Qualitative (n=17) notes mixed improvement and occasional worsening. AutoGluon predicts 7.54, a substantial delta, flagging a mismatch. YouTube views are 8. A pre‑hold or gating step is advisable.
X-MC-L3.sphere_plain: Mixed results; some love it, others find it ineffective.

sphere_commentary.overview:
Across the three series the data show distinct efficacy patterns that generally align with the intended protocol architectures.
• HEALING delivers the most consistent outcomes (mean ≈ 7.0, lowest variance) across 12 DESCENT designs.
• THRIVING’s HOLD protocols cluster in the moderate‑high range, with notable peaks in Focus & Clarity.
• TRANSFORMING’s ASCENT arcs show the widest spread and lowest mean, reflecting the challenge of shifting low‑affect states.
• YouTube reach is uniformly low, limiting engagement signals, while AutoGluon predictions add nuanced expectations for several protocols.
sphere_commentary.cross_series:
Series‑level outcomes map onto design intent while highlighting zones for refinement.
• HEALING maintains steady positive scores across Anxiety, Crisis, Over‑stimulation, and Sleep, with 63‑67% positive ratings.
• THRIVING’s Hold protocols reach the highest means for Focus (7.63) but display variability in Creative Flow.
• TRANSFORMING’s Ascents exhibit the greatest variance (σ ≈ 1.9) and include both the lowest mean (5.70) and highest predicted scores (8.23), suggesting mixed listener suitability.
sphere_commentary.anomalies:
A few protocols deviate from series‑wide patterns and merit focused investigation.
• H‑CR‑L1 shows lower observed mean (6.35) despite qualitative reports of significant improvement.
• X‑MC‑L3 records a low mean (5.70) with high dispersion, indicating a possible population split.
• T‑FC‑L3 delivers a high mean (7.63) but an unusually wide spread, hinting at a polarized responder group.
• Several protocols exhibit early peaks at score 9 exceeding score 10, warranting examination of response scaling.
sphere_commentary.development_signals:
Targeted adjustments could improve efficacy and listener alignment.
• [CONVERGENT SIGNAL] X‑ME‑L1 (n=39) – promote as a universal entry protocol across zones; explore L2/L3 variants.
• [CONVERGENT SIGNAL] X‑MD‑L3 (n=13) – split into gentler and steeper ascent versions to resolve bimodal outcomes.
• [CONVERGENT SIGNAL] X‑MC‑L3 (n=10) – investigate population mismatch; consider adding a pre‑hold phase before descent.
• [CONVERGENT SIGNAL] H‑CR‑L1 (n=43) – create a micro‑duration crisis protocol (2‑3 min) for immediate stabilization.
• [CONVERGENT SIGNAL] T‑FC‑L3 (n=35) – develop an extended‑hold (20 + min) version for deep‑work sessions.
• [CONVERGENT SIGNAL] Increase the hold phase for X‑MD‑L1 (n=22) to enhance predicted low‑affect outcomes.
sphere_commentary.embedding_analysis:
Embedding drift signals suggest evolving analytical framing, while clustering remains unavailable.
• CROSS‑RUN DRIFT: 26 of 30 protocols show similarity < 0.85, with X‑MD‑L3 (0.507) most affected.
• CROSS‑PROTOCOL SEMANTIC CLUSTERS: Insufficient embeddings for meaningful clustering.
• Confidence: Drift analysis is directional only; limited runs prevent firm conclusions about instability.
